libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Generated tests should prefer public Guice APIs such as Injector, AbstractModule, @Inject bindings, providers, and AOP interceptors rather than carrying forward old tests that import unshaded internal classes. In 2.9.1 many internal utility/CGLIB classes in the main jar are jarjar-relocated to $-prefixed names, which explains the javac failures for the old scaffold.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8858 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.sonatype.sisu:sisu-guice:2.9.1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: The 2.9.1 artifact also publishes nodeps/noaop classifiers, but the normal main artifact is the one Gradle resolves for the library coordinate; relying on classifier-only internals would not be a normal setup requirement.
Optional/test-only upstream dependencies such as slf4j-api, Spring, bnd, and Felix are only needed for specialized upstream tests, not for basic Guice coverage.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra dependency, Docker image, environment variable, or system property is required for normal meaningful coverage of sisu-guice 2.9.1; the default artifact already brings its normal runtime dependencies and embeds jarjar-shaded ASM/CGLIB classes.
